overview.title: Hermes Control Center
overview.description: A product-grade web platform for Hermes Agent: workspace-first UX, replayable autonomous runs, approvals, observability, and multilingual operations from the start.
overview.thesisBody1: Hermes already has a strong runtime, session store, tool surface, and an emerging admin UI. This project extends that base into a product-grade control center rather than rewriting Hermes from scratch.
overview.thesisBody2: The goal is to combine the best ideas from agent workbenches, operational dashboards, and collaborative run review systems into one cohesive web experience.
overview.runtimePulseDescription: The shell now tracks shared product objects for sessions, runs, approvals, and replay events before live backend adapters land.

focusAreas.workspaceFirstBody: Move beyond a flat chat surface into project-scoped workspaces, reusable presets, and long-lived execution context.
focusAreas.replayableRunsBody: Represent tool calls, approvals, browser sessions, and process events as durable timeline entries that can be inspected after the fact.
focusAreas.operationalReviewBody: Support approval inboxes, release discipline, CI signals, and run-state monitoring in the same product shell.

milestones.foundationDetail: routing, i18n, docs, CI, project shell

workspaces.title: Workspaces
workspaces.description: Review workspace-scoped repository context, runtime workload, and operator handoff without leaving the shared product shell.
workspaces.selectedWorkspaceBody: Workspace review surface for repository context, policy posture, and runtime handoff.
workspaces.runQueueBody_one: {{count}} run currently linked to this workspace.
workspaces.runQueueBody_other: {{count}} runs currently linked to this workspace.
workspaces.approvalsQueueBody_one: {{count}} pending approval currently linked to this workspace.
workspaces.approvalsQueueBody_other: {{count}} pending approvals currently linked to this workspace.
workspaces.emptyStateBody: Connect runtime-backed workspace records or fixture data to populate this review surface.

runs.title: Runs
runs.description: Runs are product-facing execution attempts inside a session. The shell now renders a shared contract for run state, approvals, artifacts, and replay events.
runs.replaySummaryEmptyBody: This run does not have replay events yet, so counts and latest event time remain empty.
runs.emptyApprovals: No approvals linked to this run.
runs.emptyTimeline: No replay events are available for this run yet.
runs.emptyStateBody: Connect a runtime adapter or start a session-backed run to populate this review surface.
runs.emptyWorkspaceStateTitle: No runs are linked to this workspace yet
runs.emptyWorkspaceStateBody: {{workspace}} does not currently expose any runs in the shared runtime snapshot.

approvals.title: Approvals
approvals.description: Approvals are durable policy records with scope, actor, expiry, and audit metadata — not one-off modal prompts.
approvals.emptyStateBody: Pending review requests will appear here once runtime policy events are connected.
approvals.emptyWorkspaceStateTitle: No approvals are linked to {{workspace}} yet
approvals.emptyWorkspaceStateBody: {{workspace}} does not currently expose approval records in the shared runtime snapshot.
approvals.relatedRunMissingBody: The related run for this approval is not currently available in the shared runtime snapshot.

sessions.title: Sessions
sessions.description: Inspect live Hermes sessions, review transcript history, and hand off from session state into the shared runtime run surface.
sessions.explorerSummary: Search, expand, and review recent sessions.
sessions.emptyStateBody: Start a conversation to see it here.
sessions.runtimeHandoffEmpty: This session does not yet map to a derived runtime run in the shared snapshot.
sessions.noPreview: No preview is available for this session yet.
sessions.runtimeHandoffPending: Open a session review route to hydrate runtime linkage and handoff metrics.

run-runtime-contract.title: Define runtime contract foundation
run-runtime-contract.summary: Documenting product-facing runtime entities and wiring the Runs/Approvals shell to shared fixtures.

run-approval-review.title: Review and promote repository governance PR
run-approval-review.summary: Merged the repository-governance work into develop after validating the branch state and CI status.

approval-push-develop.title: Promote runtime contract work for review
approval-push-develop.reason: Open-source maintainer flow requires a reviewable branch and PR before changes land in develop.

approval-repo-governance-merge.title: Merge repository governance PR
approval-repo-governance-merge.reason: The contribution guide and templates were verified as ready for develop promotion.
approval-repo-governance-merge.resolutionNote: Merged locally into develop and pushed after confirming the branch diff was documentation-only.

evt-runtime-1.title: Runtime contract task started
evt-runtime-1.detail: The scheduled maintainer run selected issue #2 as the highest-value next increment.

evt-runtime-2.title: Repository state inspection
evt-runtime-2.detail: Checked git status, branches, recent commits, roadmap, devlog, open PRs, and issues before making changes.

evt-runtime-3.title: Implementation plan written
evt-runtime-3.detail: Saved a task-level plan for the runtime contract foundation increment under docs/plans.

evt-runtime-4.title: Contract document updated
evt-runtime-4.detail: Defined workspace, session, run, timeline, approval, and artifact entities with an integration mapping back to Hermes surfaces.

evt-runtime-5.title: Review gate queued
evt-runtime-5.detail: A pending governance approval tracks the branch and PR promotion step for the runtime contract changes.

evt-governance-1.title: PR #6 diff reviewed
evt-governance-1.detail: Compared the repository-governance branch against develop and confirmed a documentation-only increment.

evt-governance-2.title: Governance merge approved
evt-governance-2.detail: The repository governance increment was promoted into develop as a coherent baseline hardening step.

evt-governance-3.title: Develop updated
evt-governance-3.detail: Squash-merged repository governance changes locally and pushed develop to keep the repository coherent for the next task branch.

ops.title: Live project progress board
ops.description: A minimal owner-facing board showing what Hermes is doing now, how many worker lanes are active, and what each autonomous employee is responsible for.
ops.activityWindowBody: This board is intentionally simple: it exposes the controller lane, active subagents, automation lanes, and the current sprint focus in one place.
ops.ownerViewBody: Use this page as the fastest way to see who is working, what is being built, and whether the autonomous project machine is still moving.

metrics.totalEmployeesDetail: All active human-like lanes, subagents, and automation workers currently modeled for this project.
metrics.activeSubagentsDetail: Independent coding/review lanes currently assigned to implementation or verification work.
metrics.activeWorkersDetail: People or automation lanes currently running, reviewing, or reporting.
metrics.automationLanesDetail: Cron/reporting/release-style workers maintaining continuity without waiting for manual input.

appShell.footerTagline: Independent OSS incubation for Hermes Agent

runtimeHydration.loadingBody: Loading sessions and replay data from the Hermes backend before rendering route-specific runtime details.
